title: NAO staining
steps: Incubate Enterococcus faecalis at 37 o C overnight in 10mL of Brain Heart Infusion (BHI) broth. Started with an isolated colony.
The next morning check OD 600 of overnight culture. Should be around ~1.5.
Diluted overnight culture to 0.01 using fresh 10mL BHI broth.
Incubate at 37 o C without shaking.
After ~3 hours check OD of cultures. Looking for mid-long phase OD 600 ~0.4. Bile will need its own dilution blank. Supplements may need more than 3 hours of incubation.
Once the cultures have hit OD 600 ~0.4, centrifuge cells at 4 o C for 5 minutes at 3500 RPM.
Re-suspend in equal volumes of 1x PBS. (if 10mL culture, add 10mL of PBS).
Take a 1mL aliquot of re-suspended culture and stain with 12.5ug of 10 -2 NAO (100mg in mL) = 12.5uL. NAO is diluted with DMSO.
Incubate at RT for ~30 minutes in the dark.
Flood 1mL of cells/NAO with ~4mL of 1xPBS. Centrifuge at 4 o C for 5 minutes at 3500 RPM.
Pour off supernatant - flood with 1x PBS - centrifuge.
Take 8uL of stained cells and put on slide - take to microscope.
creator: Eadewunm
